name: Cccs, A Division Of Triangle Family Services, Inc.
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor serving Triangle area families with emergency rental assistance, eviction prevention, and financial counseling since 1937.
description_long:
Triangle Family Services (TFS) is a established nonprofit organization founded in 1937 that operates as a HUD-approved housing counseling agency. The organization serves families in crisis across the Triangle region of North Carolina, with a comprehensive mission focused on strengthening families and building community resilience. CCCS operates as a division within this larger family services umbrella.

TFS offers direct financial assistance through multiple housing-focused programs, including emergency housing assistance, the Rental Assistance Housing Program (RAHP) for individuals experiencing homelessness with diagnosed disabilities, street outreach, and homelessness diversion services. Beyond housing, the organization provides financial counseling and coaching to help families manage money and avoid crisis. All housing services operate through Wake County's Coordinated Entry system, which determines eligibility and referrals. The organization utilizes a Housing First model—prioritizing quick placement into permanent housing before layering additional support services.

TFS distinguishes itself through its integrated service model combining direct financial assistance with professional case management and financial education. As a HUD-approved agency, the organization meets federal standards for housing counseling. Their RAHP program specifically targets individuals experiencing chronic homelessness with disabilities, pairing rental assistance with assertive case management. The organization also operates mental health services, domestic violence intervention programs, and supervised visitation services, reflecting a holistic approach to family stability.

The primary caveat is accessibility: Housing services require intake assessment through Wake County's Coordinated Entry system rather than direct application, limiting availability to referred individuals within that system. Services are geographically limited to Wake County and the Triangle area. While financial counseling is offered, the primary focus remains housing assistance rather than comprehensive credit repair or debt management.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency meeting federal standards
Direct rental and emergency housing assistance available, not just counseling
Integrated case management pairing financial assistance with ongoing support
Rental Assistance Housing Program (RAHP) specifically serves chronically homeless individuals with disabilities
Housing First model prioritizes rapid placement into permanent housing
Serves families in crisis since 1937—long-established, data-driven organization
Free financial counseling and coaching services included with housing programs
cons: Services limited to Wake County and Triangle area—not available nationwide
Housing assistance requires referral through Wake County Coordinated Entry system; cannot apply directly
All access sites are in-person only, creating barriers for some applicants
Primary focus is housing crisis intervention rather than comprehensive financial or credit services
RAHP program limited to individuals with diagnosed disabilities experiencing homelessness
best_for: Families facing eviction or housing crisis in Wake County, North Carolina
Individuals experiencing homelessness with diagnosed disabilities seeking permanent supportive housing
Low-income families needing emergency rental assistance and case management support
People seeking free financial counseling alongside housing stability programs
diagnosis: Best for Wake County families in active housing crisis seeking emergency rental assistance paired with case management and financial counseling. Primary caveat: requires in-person intake through Wake County's Coordinated Entry system and is not a direct-application service; primarily serves those meeting homelessness or eviction crisis thresholds rather than providing general financial or credit services.
services: Rental Assistance Housing Program (RAHP) for chronically homeless individuals with disabilities
Rental assistance referrals through Coordinated Entry system
Connection to community resources and emergency shelters
Assertive engagement and ongoing support services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
